2604.08524.title: What Drives Representation Steering? A Mechanistic Case Study on Steering Refusal
2604.08524.paper_summary: Applying steering vectors to large language models (LLMs) is an efficient and effective model alignment technique, but we lack an interpretable explanation for how it works-- specifically, what internal mechanisms steering vectors affect and how this results in different model outputs. To investigate the causal mechanisms underlying the effectiveness of steering vectors, we conduct a comprehensive case study on refusal. We propose a multi-token activation patching framework and discover that different steering methodologies leverage functionally interchangeable circuits when applied at the same layer. These circuits reveal that steering vectors primarily interact with the attention mechanism through the OV circuit while largely ignoring the QK circuit-- freezing all attention scores during steering drops performance by only 8.75% across two model families. A mathematical decomposition of the steered OV circuit further reveals semantically interpretable concepts, even in cases where the steering vector itself does not. Leveraging the activation patching results, we show that steering vectors can be sparsified by up to 90-99% while retaining most performance, and that different steering methodologies agree on a subset of important dimensions.
2604.08524.paper_summary_zh: 應用引導向量於大型語言模型（LLMs）是一種高效且有效的模型對齊技術，但我們缺乏對其運作方式的可解釋說明——具體而言，引導向量影響了哪些內部機制，以及這如何導致不同的模型輸出。為了調查引導向量有效性背後的因果機制，我們對拒絕進行了全面的案例研究。我們提出了一個多標記激活修補框架，並發現不同的引導方法在應用於同一層時利用了功能上可互換的電路。這些電路顯示，引導向量主要通過OV電路與注意力機制互動，同時在很大程度上忽略了QK電路——在引導過程中凍結所有注意力分數使得性能僅下降8.75%，這是在兩個模型系列中觀察到的。對引導的OV電路進行數學分解進一步揭示了語義可解釋的概念，即使在引導向量本身不提供的情況下。利用激活修補結果，我們展示了引導向量可以在保留大部分性能的情況下稀疏化高達90-99%，並且不同的引導方法在一組重要維度上達成一致。
2604.08524.author: Stephen Cheng et.al.
2604.08524.authors: Stephen Cheng, Sarah Wiegreffe, Dinesh Manocha
